IDirect3DDevice8::BeginState

Allows the title to write state-setting commands directly into Direct3D's primary push-buffer. Unlike IDirect3DDevice8::BeginPush, BeginState cannot be used to write rendering commands to the push-buffer.

HRESULT BeginState(
    DWORD Count,
    DWORD **ppPush
);

Parameters

Count
[in] The number of DWORDS to be reserved in the push-buffer. The caller can use less than this amount, but Direct3D will always guarantee that there are at least Count DWORDs free when BeginPush returns. For best performance, Count should be less than the KickOffSize, as specified by IDirect3D8::SetPushBufferSize. The caller must never write more data than space has been reserved for.
ppPush
[out] Address of a pointer to a DWORD. On return, *ppPush points directly into Direct3D's primary push-buffer.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, an error code otherwise.

Remarks

Calls to IDirect3DDevice8::SetVertexShaderConstant can be replaced by calls to BeginState and EndState. BeginState and EndState are useful for cases when constant data has just been computed using SSE. The title can then write the data directly from the SSE registers into the push-buffer (instead of writing the data to memory, having Direct3D immediately read that memory, and then writing the data back out to the GPU). After calling BeginState, IDirect3DDevice8::EndState must be called once the data is completely written.

Direct3D performs no validation on the data written to the push-buffer, even on debug builds. However, the GPU may generate a Graphics Hardware Error if the data is bad.

The following example code shows how BeginState/EndState may be used to replace a call to SetVertexShaderConstant:

// The following code replaces pDev->SetVertexShaderConstant(CONSTANT, &mat, 4):
 	
DWORD* pPush;

// Can reserve more than needed, but a value of 128 or more dwords is more 
// expensive:

pDev->BeginState(64, &pPush);

// 1 here isn't the parameter for SET_TRANSFORM_CONSTANT_LOAD; rather, it's 
// the number of dwords written to that register:

pPush[0] = D3DPUSH_ENCODE(D3DPUSH_SET_TRANSFORM_CONSTANT_LOAD, 1); 

// Here is the actual parameter for SET_TRANSFORM_CONSTANT_LOAD.  Always
// add 96 to the constant register:

pPush[1] = CONSTANT + 96; 

// A 4x4 matrix is 16 dwords.  You can encode a maximum of 32 dwords 
// to D3DPUSH_SET_TRANSFORM_CONSTANT before needing another
// D3DPUSH_ENCODE(D3DPUSH_SET_TRANSFORM_CONSTANT, ...):

pPush[2] = D3DPUSH_ENCODE(D3DPUSH_SET_TRANSFORM_CONSTANT, 16); 
memcpy(pPush + 3, &mat, sizeof(mat));

// The EndState parameter must always point to exactly the end of the
// data written, otherwise random Graphics Hardware Errors will result:

pDev->EndState(pPush + 19);

Requirements

Header: Declared in D3d8.h.

Import Library: Use D3d8.lib.

See Also

IDirect3DDevice8::EndState, IDirect3DDevice8::BeginPush, IDirect3DDevice8::EndPush
